description_long: This is a bulbous, narrow-necked ceramic vase with a flared rim and a rounded base, featuring intricate geometric patterns and stylized human figures painted in earthy tones of red, brown, and cream. The surface is adorned with zigzag and square motifs, bordered by bands of repeating lines and shapes. One side of the vase is heavily damaged, with a large section of the body broken away, exposing the hollow interior and rough ceramic edges. The painted decoration is partially worn, especially near the damaged area, but remains vivid and detailed on the intact surfaces. The neck and rim are relatively well-preserved, with some minor chipping visible. This vase appears to be an artifact, possibly of historical or cultural significance, and would likely be displayed in a protected setting.
description: A bulbous, narrow-necked ceramic vase with geometric and figurative patterns in red, brown, and cream, featuring a large broken section on one side.
description_view_0: Front view shows the intact side with detailed geometric and figurative patterns, and a mostly preserved neck and rim.
description_view_1: Side view reveals a large broken section, exposing the interior and showing significant surface wear.
description_view_2: Rear view focuses on the extensive breakage, with the hollow interior and jagged ceramic edges clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the decorated surface and a smaller chipped area near the base.
